description_long: This object is a large, rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface. The grooves are deep and create a striped pattern, giving the block a visually striking appearance. The sides of the block are solid and unadorned, with a dark, almost black coloration, while the top surface reveals a more textured, stone-like material with earthy brown and gray tones. The grooves suggest it may serve a functional purpose, such as drainage or anti-slip in an urban environment. The block appears robust and heavy, likely intended as a barrier or obstacle in public spaces. There are no visible markings, engravings, or signs of significant weathering on the surfaces.
description: A heavy, rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface and solid, dark sides. The top has a textured, earthy appearance, while the grooves create a striped pattern.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the block and the deep, parallel grooves running from left to right.
description_view_1: Side-angled view highlighting the depth and spacing of the grooves, with the block's length receding into the background.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the grooves' uniformity and the block's solid, rectangular form.
description_view_3: Opposite side-angled view, again emphasizing the groove pattern and the block's substantial, monolithic appearance.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or seating element
attribute_installation_method: embedded in concrete or set flush with pavement
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; surfaces appear clean and well-maintained with no chips or erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
